location.office_time_required: within commuting distance of one of our office hubs

level_signals: Reports to a Senior Director of Product
Expected to own international growth roadmap and country scorecards end-to-end

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: 6+ years of product management experience, including substantial time as a Growth PM or similar role (e.g., owning acquisition, activation, engagement, or monetization in a consumer or marketplace product).
must_haves.qualifier: including substantial time as a Growth PM or similar role

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Proven track record driving measurable growth in a high‑velocity consumer product or marketplace—owning end‑to‑end funnels, setting targets, and using experimentation to move core KPIs.

Experience scaling international (non‑US) markets, such as: launching or growing products in multiple countries, adapting product experiences to local behaviors, regulations, or infrastructure, and partnering with regional teams to build repeatable playbooks.

Strong data and experimentation skills: comfortable defining metrics, digging into country‑level analytics and scorecards, designing experiments, and using both quantitative and qualitative insights to inform decisions.
Demonstrated ability to work in ambiguous, fast‑paced environments, making sound decisions with imperfect information, and balancing speed, risk, and quality on complex multi‑team initiatives.

Excellent cross‑functional leadership and communication, with experience partnering closely with Engineering, Design, Data Science, UXR, BizOps, CX/Trust, Logistics, Payments/Tax, and regional go‑to‑market teams.
High empathy for international users and stakeholders, with a strong desire to build trustworthy, transparent, and locally resonant experiences for buyers and sellers.

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Experience working as a PM in a marketplace or multi‑sided platform is preferred but not required.

scope.surface: International growth product experience across Whatnot's non-US markets (acquisition, activation, engagement, retention, monetization)
scope.business_model: B2C/B2B marketplace, livestream commerce, two-sided platform
primary_metrics_inferred: Acquisition, activation, retention, and monetization metrics per country
core_responsibilities: Own the international growth vision and roadmap across geos
Drive systemic unlocks and continuously reduce chronic friction across multiple markets
Champion a hub-and-spoke model for international product needs
Own international growth metrics and country scorecards
Design and run experiments across the funnel
Translate regional expertise into scalable productized playbooks
Ensure product is global-ready by default, including i18n, localization, and regulatory compliance

compensation.equity_form: not specified beyond mention of equity being part of total compensation (implied by startup context)

green_flags: Clear product scope and ownership: international growth roadmap across all non-US markets
Explicit reporting structure (Senior Director of Product)
Broad list of cross-functional partners named, indicating organizational maturity
Dogfooding budget included as benefit — signals user-centric culture
Company recognized as #1 Best Startup Employer in America by Forbes
Explicitly states marketplace PM experience is 'preferred but not required', lowering bar for adjacent backgrounds
red_flags: Requires commuting distance to one of five US cities or London — limits truly remote candidates despite 'remote-first' framing
Visa sponsorship status not mentioned anywhere in JD
No specific headcount or team size provided for the international product org
Equity form and amount not specified
